evals.name: Full-stack SaaS scaffold with auth and database
evals.prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 15 SaaS app with App Router route groups, Auth.js v5 GitHub OAuth, Drizzle ORM + Neon PostgreSQL, Server Actions, middleware protection, and Vercel deployment.
evals.expected_output: Complete scaffold with auth config, middleware, drizzle schema, server actions, next.config.ts, and Playwright auth tests

assertions.text: Output includes middleware.ts protecting /dashboard routes
Output includes auth.config.ts as edge-compatible (no DB adapter)
Output includes drizzle schema with users and accounts tables
Output includes Server Action with Zod validation before DB operation
Output includes next.config.ts with output: 'standalone'
Output includes security headers in next.config.ts
Output includes src/env.ts with split server/client Zod schemas
Output uses App Router route groups (auth), (dashboard), (marketing)
Output does NOT use Pages Router (getServerSideProps, pages/ directory)
Output includes Playwright test for unauthenticated redirect

evals.name: Server vs Client Component boundary decisions
evals.prompt: This page has a static sidebar nav, a real-time counter updated via WebSocket, a data table that fetches from the database, and a theme toggle button. Which components should be Server Components and which should be Client Components?
evals.expected_output: Component boundary analysis with Server/Client classification and rationale for each component

assertions.text: Sidebar nav classified as Server Component
Real-time counter classified as Client Component
Data table fetching from DB classified as Server Component
Theme toggle classified as Client Component
Output provides rationale for each classification
Output does NOT recommend use client on layout files

evals.name: Pages Router to App Router migration plan
evals.prompt: Plan migration of 8 pages using getServerSideProps and a custom _app.tsx with Redux + QueryClient providers to Next.js App Router. Team needs incremental migration with zero downtime.
evals.expected_output: Phased migration plan with getServerSideProps patterns, provider migration, and useRouter replacement guide

assertions.text: Output confirms pages/ and app/ can coexist during migration
Output maps getServerSideProps to Server Components pattern
Output documents useRouter from next/router → next/navigation migration
Output explains how to move providers from _app.tsx to app/layout.tsx
Output provides a migration order (lower risk pages first)

evals.name: Security audit of Next.js scaffold
evals.prompt: Audit this Next.js project: no security headers, AUTH_SECRET is 8 characters, NEXT_PUBLIC_DATABASE_URL exposes DB credentials, Server Action has no input validation, output is not standalone.
evals.expected_output: Security audit with [FAIL] items for each vulnerability and specific fixes

assertions.text: Output flags AUTH_SECRET length as [FAIL]
Output flags NEXT_PUBLIC_DATABASE_URL as [FAIL] — secrets must not be NEXT_PUBLIC_
Output flags missing Server Action Zod validation as [FAIL]
Output flags missing security headers as [WARN] or [FAIL]
Output provides specific fix for each [FAIL] item

evals.name: Partial Pre-rendering with streaming
evals.prompt: Design a product page using Next.js 15 PPR: static shell with product name and images served from CDN instantly, dynamic inventory count and recommended products streamed from server.
evals.expected_output: PPR-designed page with Suspense boundaries, loading skeletons, and explanation of static vs dynamic parts

assertions.text: Output uses Suspense to wrap dynamic inventory section
Output uses Suspense to wrap recommended products section
Output explains that static shell (name, images) is served from CDN
Output includes loading.tsx or inline fallback skeleton
Output references experimental ppr: true in next.config.ts
